questions.question: Which property initializes or creates a CSS counter?
questions.explanation: counter-reset creates a new counter (or resets an existing one) to a specified initial value, typically 0.

questions.question: Where must counter() be used to display a value?
options: In the content property of ::before or ::after
questions.explanation: counter() and counters() functions can only be used inside the content property of ::before or ::after pseudo-elements.

questions.question: What does counters(name, '.') do differently from counter(name)?
options: Displays nested counter hierarchy with separator
questions.explanation: counters() joins all counter instances from the current element up through its ancestors using the specified separator (e.g., '1.2.3').

questions.question: What value does counter-increment add by default if no increment is specified?
questions.explanation: counter-increment increases the counter by 1 by default. You can specify a different increment: counter-increment: section 2.

questions.question: Can CSS counters work on elements with display: none?
questions.explanation: Elements with display: none are not rendered, so their counters are not incremented. visibility: hidden elements DO increment counters.

questions.question: What is the default starting value of a counter created by counter-reset?
questions.explanation: By default, counter-reset initializes a counter to 0. You can specify a different starting value: counter-reset: section 5.

questions.question: Which CSS function produces output like '1.1.1' for deep nesting?
questions.explanation: counters(counter-name, '.') automatically walks up the DOM tree, collecting the counter value at each level and joining with dots.

questions.question: How do you display a counter in Roman numerals?
questions.explanation: The second parameter of counter() specifies the display style. counter(section, upper-roman) displays as I, II, III.

questions.question: What happens to counter scope when a nested element uses counter-reset?
options: A new counter instance is created for that subtree
questions.explanation: Each element with counter-reset creates a new counter instance scoped to that element and its descendants. The parent counter is still accessible via counters().

questions.question: How do you create a countdown using CSS counters?
options: Countdowns are not possible with CSS
questions.explanation: Set counter-reset to 11 (one more than desired start), then use counter-increment: -1 to count down from 10.

questions.question: Which is the correct CSS to number headings as 'Section 1:', 'Section 2:', etc.?
questions.explanation: The counter is incremented on h2 and displayed via ::before with custom text formatting in the content property.

questions.question: What property prevents a list item's default marker from appearing when using custom counters?
questions.explanation: list-style: none removes the default list markers (bullets or numbers), allowing custom counter-based numbering without interference.

questions.question: How can you number figures as 'Figure 1', 'Figure 2', etc. in a document?
options: counter-reset on body, counter-increment on figure::before
Use an ordered list for figures
questions.explanation: Set counter-reset: figure on the body, then use figure figcaption::before { counter-increment: figure; content: 'Figure ' counter(figure) ': '; }.

questions.question: What browser support do CSS counters have?
questions.explanation: CSS counters have been supported since Internet Explorer 8 and all modern browsers, making them one of the most widely supported CSS features.

questions.question: What happens when two counters use the same name in different parts of the page?
options: They remain independent if scoped by different counter-reset elements
questions.explanation: Each element with counter-reset creates a new scoped instance. Counters with the same name in different scope trees are independent.

questions.question: What is the @counter-style rule used for?
questions.explanation: @counter-style allows you to define fully custom numbering patterns (symbols, cyclic, etc.) for use with CSS counters and list-style-type.

questions.question: How do you skip numbering on specific items in a counter sequence?
options: Use counter-increment: 0 on the skipped item
Set display: none on the item
questions.explanation: Setting counter-increment: 0 on an item keeps it from advancing the counter while still displaying the current value.

questions.question: What is the key advantage of CSS counters over manual numbering in HTML?
options: Numbers automatically adjust when content is added or removed
questions.explanation: CSS counters automatically renumber when content is inserted, removed, or reordered, eliminating manual HTML updates.

questions.question: What does counter-increment do when applied to the ::before pseudo-element?
options: Increments the counter before displaying it
Increments the counter after displaying it
questions.explanation: Counter increment in ::before happens before the element content renders, ensuring the displayed value includes the current increment.

questions.question: What is the CSS output of counter(section, lower-greek)?
questions.explanation: lower-greek displays the counter using lowercase Greek letters (α, β, γ). This is one of many counter styles available.
